volumeTitle: Casina. The Casket Comedy. Curculio. Epidicus. The Two Menaechmuses
label: Epidicus (Section 2.2.305c - Section 3.3.407c)

sections.label: Section 2.2.305c
sections.original: I , numera , nil ego te moror .
sections.translation: Go and count it out ; I ’ ll not detain you at all .

sections.label: Section 2.3.306
sections.original: nullum esse opinor ego agrum in agro Attico
sections.translation: I do think that in the Attic land there is no spot of land so fertile as is this Periphanes of ours ; why , from the locked and sealed-up money-chest I summon forth silver just as much as I please .

sections.label: Section 2.3.310
sections.original: quod pol ego metuo si senex resciverit ,
sections.translation: But this , i ’ troth , I am afraid of , that if the old man should come to know it , he ’ ll be making parasites of elm-twigs [*] , to be shaving me quite clean . But one matter and consideration disturbs me — what music-girl , one that goes out on hire , I ’ m to show to Apaecides . ( He muses .) And that as well I ’ ve got : this morning the old gentleman bade me

sections.label: Section 2.3.315
sections.original: conducere aliquam fidicinam sibi huc domum ,
sections.translation: bring for him on hire some music-girl to his house here , to sing for him while he was performing a sacrifice [*] . She shall be hired , and be instructed beforehand in what way she ’ s to prove herself cunning towards the old man . I ’ ll away in-doors ; I ’ ll get the money out of the swindled old fellow .

sections.label: Section 3.1.320
sections.original: Expectando éxedor míser atque exénteror ,
sections.translation: I ’ m distractedly in suspense and worn to the heart with waiting how the fair promises of Epidicus will turn out for me . I ’ ve been tormented too long . Whether there is to be anything , or whether there is not , I wish to know .

sections.label: Section 3.1.323b
sections.original: Per illám tibi cópiam
sections.translation: For all these resources you may still seek some other resources for yourself . For my part , I knew at the first , or the instant , that there was no help for you in him [*] .

sections.label: Section 3.1.325b
sections.original: Interii hércle ego .
sections.translation: Upon my faith , I ’ m ruined !

sections.label: Section 3.1.326
sections.original: Ábsurde facis qui ángas te animi ; si hércle ego illum semel préndero ,
sections.translation: You act absurdly in tormenting yourself in mind . By my troth , if I should catch him , I would never allow that slave of a fellow to be laughing at us with impunity .

sections.label: Section 3.1.328
sections.original: Quíd illum facere vís , qui , tibi quoi dívitiae domi máxumae sunt ,
sections.translation: What can you expect him to do , you , who have such great wealth at home , and have not a coin of it , as you say , and have in yourself no resources for your friend ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.331
sections.original: Si hercle hábeam , polliceár lubens , verum áliquid aliqua aliquó modo
sections.translation: I ’ faith , if I had had it , I should have proffered it with pleasure ; but something in some manner [*] , in some way , in some direction , from some person , some hope I have for you , that there ’ ll be some good fortune for you to share with me .

sections.label: Section 3.1.333
sections.original: Vaé tibi , muricide homo .
sections.translation: Woe to you , you sneaking fellow [*] .

sections.label: Section 3.1.333b
sections.original: Qui tibi lubet mihi male loqui ?
sections.translation: Why does it please you to abuse me ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.334
sections.original: Quippe tú mi aliquíd aliquo modo alicunde ab aliquibus blatis
sections.translation: Why , because you are prating to me about something in some manner , from somewhere or other , from some persons ,

sections.label: Section 3.1.335
sections.original: quod nusquamst neque ego id inmitto in aures meas
sections.translation: that nowhere exists , and I won ’ t admit it to my ears . Of no more assistance are you unto me than he who never yet has been born .

sections.label: Section 3.2.337
sections.original: Fecísti iam officium tuom , me meum nunc facere oportet .
sections.translation: You ’ ve done your duty then ; it now befits me to do mine . Through this care of mine , you may be allowed to be at ease . ( In a lower voice .) This , in fact , is now lost to you ; don ’ t at all be setting your hopes on it . ( Holding some of the coins in his hands .) How very shining it is ! You only trust me for that .

sections.label: Section 3.2.340
sections.original: crede modo mihi : sic ego ago , sic egerunt nostri .
sections.translation: This way I ’ m going to act , this way my forefathers have acted before me . O ye immortal Gods , what a brilliant day you have bestowed upon me in this how propitious and how favourable to my requests ! But why do I delay to take my departure hence , that I may bear this supply with lucky auspices to the colony [*] . I ’ m delaying while I ’ m standing here . But what means this ? Before the house I see the two companions ,

sections.label: Section 3.2.345
sections.original: erum et Chaéribulum , conspicor . quid hic ágitis ? accipe hoc sis .
sections.translation: my master and Chaeribulus . ( Accosting them .) What are you doing ? Take this , will you .

sections.label: Section 3.2.346
sections.original: Quantum hic inest ?
sections.translation: How much is there in this ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.346b
sections.original: Quantum sat est , et plus satis : superfit .
sections.translation: As much as is enough , and more than enough ; a superabundance ; I ’ ve brought more by ten minae than you owe to the Banker . So long as I please and obey you , I value my own back at a straw .

sections.label: Section 3.2.349
sections.original: Nam quid ita ?
sections.translation: But why so ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.349b
sections.original: Quia ego tuom patrem faciam parenticidam .
sections.translation: Because I shall make your father a bag-murderer [*] .

sections.label: Section 3.2.350
sections.original: Quid istúc est verbi ?
sections.translation: What kind of expression is that ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.350b
sections.original: Nil moror vetera et volgata verba ;
sections.translation: I don ’ t at all care for your old-fashioned and every-day words ; you chouse by purses full [*] , but I ’ ll chouse by bags full . For the procurer took away a whole lot of money for the music-girl ( I paid it ; with these hands I counted it out ), her whom your father supposes to be his own daughter . Now , again , that your father may be deceived , and assistance be provided for you ,

sections.label: Section 3.2.355
sections.original: inveni : nam ita suasi seni atque hanc habui orationem
sections.translation: I ’ ve discovered a method . In such a way have I persuaded the old gentleman — and had a talk to this effect , that , when you returned , you might not have possession of her [*] —

sections.label: Section 3.2.356b
sections.original: Euge .
sections.translation: Bravo !— bravo !

sections.label: Section 3.2.357
sections.original: ea iam domist pro filia .
sections.translation: She ’ s now at your house [*] in place of her .

sections.label: Section 3.2.357b
sections.original: Iam teneo .
sections.translation: I understand .

sections.label: Section 3.2.357c
sections.original: Nunc cautorem
sections.translation: Now he has given me Apaecides by way of guarantee in this matter ( he ’ s waiting for me at the Forum ), as if to seem the purchaser .

sections.label: Section 3.2.359b
sections.original: Haud male .
sections.translation: Not a bad precaution !

sections.label: Section 3.2.359c
sections.original: Iam ípse cautor captust .
sections.translation: The cautious man ’ s now taken in himself ;

sections.label: Section 3.2.360
sections.original: ipse in meo cóllo tuos pater cruminam collocavit ;
sections.translation: your own father himself placed this purse around my neck [*] . He ’ s making preparation , that immediately on your arrival home you shall be married .

sections.label: Section 3.2.362
sections.original: Vno persuadebit modo , si illam , quae adducta est mecum ,
sections.translation: In one way only will he persuade me ; if Orcus takes her away from me , who has been brought with me .

sections.label: Section 3.2.363b
sections.original: Nunc ego hanc astutiam institui .
sections.translation: Now I ’ ve hit upon this scheme : I ’ ll go by myself alone to the procurer ’ s house ; I ’ ll instruct him ,

sections.label: Section 3.2.365
sections.original: si quid ad eum adveniam , ut sibi essé datum argentum dicat
sections.translation: if any one comes to him , to say that the money has been paid him for the music-girl ; inasmuch as , the day before yesterday , I paid it down with my own hands for this mistress of yours , whom your father takes to be his own daughter . Then the procurer , unknowingly , will be staking his accursed head ,

sections.label: Section 3.2.370
sections.original: quasi pro illa argentum acceperit , quae tecum adducta nunc est .
sections.translation: as though he had received the money for her who has now been brought here together with you .

sections.label: Section 3.2.371
sections.original: Vorsutior es quam rota figularis .
sections.translation: You are more versatile than a potter ’ s-wheel .

sections.label: Section 3.2.371b
sections.original: Iam ego parabo
sections.translation: Now I ’ ll get ready some artful music-girl , who ’ s hired at a didrachm , to pretend that she has been purchased , and cleverly to trick the two old fellows : Apaecides , together with her , will bring her to your father .

sections.label: Section 3.2.374b
sections.original: Vt parate .
sections.translation: How adroitly managed !

sections.label: Section 3.2.375
sections.original: Eam pérmeditatam , meis dolis astutiisque onustam
sections.translation: Her , prepared beforehand with my devices , and provided with my schemes , I shall send to him . But I ’ m talking at too great length ; you have delayed me too long : you now know these things how they are to be ; I ’ ll be off .

sections.label: Section 3.2.377b
sections.original: Bene ambulato .
sections.translation: Success attend you !

sections.label: Section 3.2.378
sections.original: Nimis dóctus ille est ad male faciendum .
sections.translation: He is very clever at artful tricks .

sections.label: Section 3.2.378b
sections.original: Me equidem certo
sections.translation: Indeed , by his plans , he has saved me , that ’ s sure .

sections.label: Section 3.2.379b
sections.original: Abeamus intro hinc ad me .
sections.translation: Let ’ s go hence into my house .

sections.label: Section 3.2.380
sections.original: Atque aliquantó lubentius quam abs te sum egressus intus ;
sections.translation: Yes , and a little more joyfully than I came out of your house , by the courage and conduct of Epidicus , do I return into camp with the spoil .

sections.label: Section 3.3.382
sections.original: Non oris causa modo homines aequom fuit
sections.translation: Not only for the sake of the face were it right for men to have a mirror for themselves wherein to look at their faces ; but one with which they might be enabled to examine the heart of discretion ,

sections.label: Section 3.3.385
sections.original: igitur perspicere ut possent cordis copiam
sections.translation: and therefore be able to examine the resources of the mind ; when they had looked in that , they might afterwards consider how they had once passed their lives in youth . Just as myself , for instance , who , for the sake of my son , began to torment myself in mind , as though my son

sections.label: Section 3.3.390
sections.original: meus déliquisset me erga aut quasi non plurima
sections.translation: had been guilty of some offence against , me , or as though my own misdeeds had not been most heavy in my youth . In truth , we old fellows are out of our senses sometimes . This , in my own opinion at least , has proved advantageous . But my friend Apaecides is coming with the spoil .

sections.label: Section 3.3.395
sections.original: venire salvom mercatorem gaudeo .
sections.translation: I ’ m glad that the negotiator has returned safe .

sections.label: Section 3.3.396
sections.original: quid fit ?
sections.translation: How goes it ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.396b
sections.original: Di deaeque te adiuvant .
sections.translation: The Gods and Goddesses are favouring you .

sections.label: Section 3.3.396c
sections.original: Omen placet .
sections.translation: The omen pleases me .

sections.label: Section 3.3.397
sections.original: Quin omini omnes suppetunt res prospere .
sections.translation: A person with whom all things go on prosperously . But do you order her to be taken in-doors .

sections.label: Section 3.3.398b
sections.original: Heus , foras
sections.translation: Hallo there ! come out of doors here , some one . ( A SERVANT comes out .) Take that woman into the house !

sections.label: Section 3.3.400
sections.original: atque audin ?
sections.translation: And , do you hear ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.400b
sections.original: Quid vis ?
sections.translation: What do you desire ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.400c
sections.original: Cave siris cum filia
sections.translation: Take care you don ’ t permit this woman to associate with my daughter , or to see her . Now do you understand ? I wish her to be shut up apart in that little chamber ; there ’ s a great difference between the manners of a maiden and a courtesan .

sections.label: Section 3.3.404
sections.original: Docte et sapienter dicis . † num nimis potest
sections.translation: You speak cleverly and judiciously ; each man cannot keep too strict a guard upon the chastity of his daughter .

sections.label: Section 3.3.406
sections.original: edepol ne ístam temperi gnato tuo
sections.translation: Upon my faith , we certainly did forestall this woman from your son just in time .

sections.label: Section 3.3.407b
sections.original: Quid iam ?
sections.translation: Why so ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.407c
sections.original: Quia dixit mihi
sections.translation: Because another person told me that he had just seen your son here .
